title: viola canina var. procera
intro: Viola canina var. procera, also known as the yellow archangel or dog violet, is a perennial plant species in the violet family (Violaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Viola canina var. procera include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of Viola canina var. procera include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
